title: Vallanadu Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Vallanadu Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Vallanadu Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area of Tamil Nadu in South India created for the protection of blackbuck antelope.
overview.description: Vallanadu Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area of Tamil Nadu in South India created for the protection of blackbuck antelope. Located on an isolated hillock in Vallanadu Village of Srivaikundam Taluk, it is the southernmost place in India where a natural population of blackbuck exists.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Deivaseyalpuram
topPlaces.description: Deivacheyalpuram, also known as Deivaseyalpuram, is a village located in Thoothukudi district of Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Aazhigudi
topPlaces.description: Aazhigudi is a village in the Tuticorin District of Tamil Nadu, India. It is 12 km from Tirunelveli, and the river Thamirabarani passes to the east.

topPlaces.name: Vittilapuram
topPlaces.description: Vittilapuram is a village in Thoothukudi district, Tamil Nadu, India. It is located on the bank of the Tamirabarani river.

topPlaces.name: Kailasanathar Temple, Srivaikuntam
topPlaces.description: Kailasanathar Temple in Srivaikuntam, a village in Thoothukudi district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva. It is located 30 km from Tirunelveli.

topPlaces.name: Srivaikuntanathan Perumal temple
topPlaces.description: Srivaikuntanathan Perumal Temple (also called Srivaikuntam temple and Kallapiran temple) in Srivaikuntam, a town in Thoothukudi district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. It is located 22 km from Tirunelveli.

topPlaces.name: Thiruvaragunamangai Perumal Temple
topPlaces.description: Thiruvaragunamangai Perumal Temple (also called Vijayasana Perumal) is one of the Nava Tirupati, the nine Hindu temples dedicated to Vishnu located in the Tiruchendur-Tirunelveli route, Tamil Nadu, India in the banks of Thamiraparani river. All these 9 temples are classified as Divya Desams, the 108 temples of Vishnu revered by the 12 poet-saints of the Sri Vaishnava tradition, called the Alvars.

topPlaces.name: Adichanallur
topPlaces.description: Adichanallur is an archaeological site in Thoothukudi district in Tamil Nadu, India that has been the site of a number of very important archaeological finds. Korkai, the capital of the Early Pandyan Kingdom, is located about 15 km from Adichanallur.

topPlaces.name: Thirupuliyangudi Perumal Temple
topPlaces.description: The Thirupuliyangudi Perumal Temple is one of the Nava Tirupati, the nine Hindu temples dedicated to Vishnu located in Tiruchendur-Tirunelveli route, Tamil Nadu, India in the banks of Thamiraparani river, in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu who is worshipped as Bhumipalar and Lakshmi as Pulingudivalli.

Srivaikuntanathan Perumal Temple (also called Srivaikuntam temple and Kallapiran temple) in Srivaikuntam, a town in Thoothukudi district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. It is located 22 km from Tirunelveli.

Thiruvaragunamangai Perumal Temple (also called Vijayasana Perumal) is one of the Nava Tirupati, the nine Hindu temples dedicated to Vishnu located in the Tiruchendur-Tirunelveli route, Tamil Nadu, India in the banks of Thamiraparani river. All these 9 temples are classified as Divya Desams, the 108 temples of Vishnu revered by the 12 poet-saints of the Sri Vaishnava tradition, called the Alvars.

Adichanallur is an archaeological site in Thoothukudi district in Tamil Nadu, India that has been the site of a number of very important archaeological finds. Korkai, the capital of the Early Pandyan Kingdom, is located about 15 km from Adichanallur.

The Thirupuliyangudi Perumal Temple is one of the Nava Tirupati, the nine Hindu temples dedicated to Vishnu located in Tiruchendur-Tirunelveli route, Tamil Nadu, India in the banks of Thamiraparani river, in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu who is worshipped as Bhumipalar and Lakshmi as Pulingudivalli.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Vallanadu Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Vallanadu Wildlife Sanctuary is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Vallanadu Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (109 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (106 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Vallanadu Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Vallanadu Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Vallanadu Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Vallanadu Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Vallanadu Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Deivaseyalpuram, Aazhigudi, Vittilapuram, Kailasanathar Temple, Srivaikuntam, Srivaikuntanathan Perumal temple.
